rent.note: furnished 1BR city centre avg ~$973; mid-range ~$750 midpoint with options

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; reasonable Czech utilities

groceries.note: home cooking; 16% of expenses per source breakdown

diningOut.note: student city; cheaper than Prague; good Czech pubs

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; growing tech scene second to Prague

simData.note: Czech SIM; T-Mobile or O2; unlimited data plans

misc.note: gym, toiletries, cultural events, entertainment, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. University city; slight summer dip when students leave.

evidence.claim: 1BR city centre avg ~$973; sharing own room in flat 10-13k CZK (~$430–$560)
evidence.quote: Renting a 1-bedroom apartment in the city centre costs an additional $973 on average. Having your own room in a shared flat is around 10-13,000 CZK monthly (approximately $430-560 USD)

evidence.claim: expenses: housing 55%, groceries 16%, utilities 15%; transit pass $26
evidence.quote: The distribution of expenses shows property takes 55%, groceries 16%, eating out 8%, transportation 2%, utilities 15%, and leisure 4%. A monthly public transport pass is typically priced at $26
You can expect to spend around $1,915 per month for 1 person. On average, a single person needs $941 per month without rent

evidence.claim: Brno lower cost than Prague; cultural events and green spaces; good for nomads
evidence.quote: Brno's lower cost of living, cultural events, and access to green spaces provide a balanced lifestyle for digital nomads who want both productivity and quality of life
